```{r}
# Build one clean drift coordinate table
# IMPORTANT: one row per drift only
# ------------------------------------------------------------

drift_coords <- event_cluster_counts %>%
  mutate(
    DriftID_clean = stringr::str_remove(EventID_clean, "_[0-9]+$")
  ) %>%
  distinct(EventID_clean, DriftID_clean, Latitude, Longitude) %>%
  group_by(DriftID_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    Latitude = mean(Latitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    Longitude = mean(Longitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_events = n_distinct(EventID_clean),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Build drift-level cluster counts
# This is the only real analytical change:
# event-level counts are summed up to drift-level counts
# ------------------------------------------------------------

drift_cluster_counts <- event_cluster_counts %>%
  mutate(
    HDBSCAN_cluster = as.character(HDBSCAN_cluster),
    DriftID_clean = stringr::str_remove(EventID_clean, "_[0-9]+$")
  ) %>%
  group_by(DriftID_clean, HDBSCAN_cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n_clicks = sum(n_clicks, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    drift_coords,
    by = "DriftID_clean"
  )

cat("Rows in drift_cluster_counts:", nrow(drift_cluster_counts), "\n")
cat("Unique drifts:", dplyr::n_distinct(drift_cluster_counts$DriftID_clean), "\n")
cat("Total clicks represented:", sum(drift_cluster_counts$n_clicks), "\n\n")

print(drift_cluster_counts, n = Inf)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Make wide drift pie tables
# These mimic the original event pie tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------

drift_pies_including_noise <- drift_cluster_counts %>%
  select(
    DriftID_clean,
    Latitude,
    Longitude,
    n_events,
    HDBSCAN_cluster,
    n_clicks
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = HDBSCAN_cluster,
    values_from = n_clicks,
    values_fill = 0,
    names_prefix = "cluster_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Compatibility with original make_pie_slice_data()
    # It expects a column named EventID.
    EventID = DriftID_clean
  )

drift_pies_excluding_noise <- drift_cluster_counts %>%
  filter(HDBSCAN_cluster != "0") %>%
  select(
    DriftID_clean,
    Latitude,
    Longitude,
    n_events,
    HDBSCAN_cluster,
    n_clicks
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = HDBSCAN_cluster,
    values_from = n_clicks,
    values_fill = 0,
    names_prefix = "cluster_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    EventID = DriftID_clean
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Pie radius
# Use fixed size so pies are comparable by composition,
# not by number of clicks.
# ------------------------------------------------------------

drift_fixed_pie_radius <- 0.45

drift_pies_including_noise <- drift_pies_including_noise %>%
  mutate(pie_radius = drift_fixed_pie_radius)

drift_pies_excluding_noise <- drift_pies_excluding_noise %>%
  mutate(pie_radius = drift_fixed_pie_radius)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save drift pie tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------

write.csv(
  drift_pies_including_noise,
  file.path(map_table_dir, "drift_pie_map_data_including_noise.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  drift_pies_excluding_noise,
  file.path(map_table_dir, "drift_pie_map_data_excluding_noise.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Drift pie map function
# Uses original manual pie-slice workflow
# ------------------------------------------------------------

make_drift_pie_map <- function(pie_data, include_noise = TRUE, output_name) {
  
  cluster_cols <- names(pie_data)[stringr::str_detect(names(pie_data), "^cluster_")]
  
  cluster_cols <- cluster_cols[
    order(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove(cluster_cols, "cluster_")))
  ]
  
  pie_slices <- make_pie_slice_data(
    pie_data = pie_data,
    cluster_cols = cluster_cols,
    radius_col = "pie_radius"
  )
  
  xlim <- range(pie_data$Longitude, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-1.5, 1.5)
  ylim <- range(pie_data$Latitude, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-1.5, 1.5)
  
  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(
      data = world,
      fill = "gray92",
      color = "gray60"
    ) +
    geom_polygon(
      data = pie_slices,
      aes(
        x = Longitude,
        y = Latitude,
        group = piece_id,
        fill = cluster
      ),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.12,
      alpha = 0.95
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = cluster_colors_consistent,
      drop = FALSE
    ) +
    coord_sf(
      xlim = xlim,
      ylim = ylim,
      expand = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(
      title = ifelse(
        include_noise,
        "Drift-Level HDBSCAN Cluster Composition: Including Noise",
        "Drift-Level HDBSCAN Cluster Composition: Excluding Noise"
      ),
      subtitle = "One pie per drift",
      x = "Longitude",
      y = "Latitude",
      fill = "HDBSCAN cluster"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(map_plot_dir, output_name),
    plot = p,
    width = 8.5,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
  return(p)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Make drift pie maps
# ------------------------------------------------------------

p_drift_map_including_noise <- make_drift_pie_map(
  pie_data = drift_pies_including_noise,
  include_noise = TRUE,
  output_name = "hdbscan_drift_pie_map_including_noise_final_colors.png"
)

p_drift_map_excluding_noise <- make_drift_pie_map(
  pie_data = drift_pies_excluding_noise,
  include_noise = FALSE,
  output_name = "hdbscan_drift_pie_map_excluding_noise_final_colors.png"
)

print(p_drift_map_including_noise)
print(p_drift_map_excluding_noise)

# ============================================================
# DRIFT-LEVEL STACKED COMPOSITION PLOTS
# Same style as original event-level stacked plots
# ============================================================

make_drift_stacked_barplot <- function(pie_data, include_noise = TRUE, output_name) {
  
  cluster_cols <- names(pie_data)[stringr::str_detect(names(pie_data), "^cluster_")]
  
  cluster_cols <- cluster_cols[
    order(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove(cluster_cols, "cluster_")))
  ]
  
  bar_data <- pie_data %>%
    arrange(Longitude) %>%
    mutate(
      Drift_label = paste0(DriftID_clean, "\n", round(abs(Longitude), 1), "°W"),
      Drift_label = factor(Drift_label, levels = Drift_label)
    ) %>%
    select(
      EventID,
      DriftID_clean,
      Drift_label,
      Longitude,
      Latitude,
      all_of(cluster_cols)
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(cluster_cols),
      names_to = "cluster",
      values_to = "n_clicks"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      cluster = stringr::str_remove(cluster, "cluster_")
    ) %>%
    group_by(EventID, DriftID_clean, Drift_label, Longitude, Latitude) %>%
    mutate(
      prop_clicks = n_clicks / sum(n_clicks)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  fill_values <- if (include_noise) {
    cluster_colors_consistent
  } else {
    cluster_colors_consistent[names(cluster_colors_consistent) != "0"]
  }
  
  p <- ggplot(
    bar_data,
    aes(
      x = Drift_label,
      y = prop_clicks,
      fill = cluster
    )
  ) +
    geom_col(
      width = 0.9,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.1
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = fill_values,
      drop = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
      expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = ifelse(
        include_noise,
        "Drift-Level HDBSCAN Cluster Composition: Including Noise",
        "Drift-Level HDBSCAN Cluster Composition: Excluding Noise"
      ),
      subtitle = "Drifts ordered west-to-east by longitude",
      x = "Drift",
      y = "Percent of analyzed clicks",
      fill = "HDBSCAN cluster"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "right"
    )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(map_plot_dir, output_name),
    plot = p,
    width = 11,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
  return(p)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Make drift stacked plots
# ------------------------------------------------------------

p_drift_bar_including_noise <- make_drift_stacked_barplot(
  pie_data = drift_pies_including_noise,
  include_noise = TRUE,
  output_name = "hdbscan_drift_stacked_composition_including_noise_final_colors.png"
)

p_drift_bar_excluding_noise <- make_drift_stacked_barplot(
  pie_data = drift_pies_excluding_noise,
  include_noise = FALSE,
  output_name = "hdbscan_drift_stacked_composition_excluding_noise_final_colors.png"
)

print(p_drift_bar_including_noise)
print(p_drift_bar_excluding_noise)

cat("\nDONE: Drift-level pie maps and stacked plots saved to:\n")
cat(map_plot_dir, "\n")

```

## Plot Groups for Crowded Areas

```{r}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Define manual ADRIFT plotting groups using coordinate zones
#
# These groups affect plotting only.
# Original DriftID_clean and HDBSCAN assignments are unchanged.
# ------------------------------------------------------------

manual_grouped_drift_counts <- drift_cluster_counts %>%
  mutate(
    PlotGroupID = case_when(
      
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      # Northern nearshore ADRIFT group
      # Around ~41N, ~124W
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      stringr::str_detect(DriftID_clean, "^ADRIFT") &
        Latitude >= 40.3 &
        Latitude <= 41.5 &
        Longitude >= -125.0 &
        Longitude <= -124.0 ~ "ADRIFT_North_41N_124W",
      
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      # Central nearshore ADRIFT group
      # Around ~38N, ~124W
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      stringr::str_detect(DriftID_clean, "^ADRIFT") &
        Latitude >= 37.3 &
        Latitude <= 38.5 &
        Longitude >= -124.2 &
        Longitude <= -123.2 ~ "ADRIFT_Central_38N_124W",
      
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      # Southern nearshore ADRIFT group
      # Around ~36N, ~122W
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      stringr::str_detect(DriftID_clean, "^ADRIFT") &
        Latitude >= 35.2 &
        Latitude <= 36.3 &
        Longitude >= -122.6 &
        Longitude <= -121.2 ~ "ADRIFT_South_36N_122W",
      
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      # Everything else stays as one pie per drift
      # ------------------------------------------------------
      TRUE ~ DriftID_clean
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Check exactly which drifts are assigned to each plot group
# ------------------------------------------------------------

manual_plot_group_membership <- manual_grouped_drift_counts %>%
  distinct(
    PlotGroupID,
    DriftID_clean,
    Latitude,
    Longitude
  ) %>%
  group_by(PlotGroupID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_drifts_in_group = n_distinct(DriftID_clean),
    drifts_in_group = paste(sort(unique(DriftID_clean)), collapse = "; "),
    group_latitude = mean(Latitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    group_longitude = mean(Longitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(group_latitude)

cat("\nManual ADRIFT plot group membership:\n")
print(manual_plot_group_membership, n = Inf)

write.csv(
  manual_plot_group_membership,
  file.path(map_table_dir, "manual_adrift_plot_group_membership_improved.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Create ONE coordinate per plotting group
# ------------------------------------------------------------

manual_group_coordinates <- manual_grouped_drift_counts %>%
  group_by(PlotGroupID) %>%
  summarise(
    Latitude = mean(Latitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    Longitude = mean(Longitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_drifts_in_group = n_distinct(DriftID_clean),
    drifts_in_group = paste(sort(unique(DriftID_clean)), collapse = "; "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Collapse cluster counts to plotting groups
# IMPORTANT:
# coordinates are added AFTER cluster summarisation
# so every cluster slice shares the SAME pie center
# ------------------------------------------------------------

manual_grouped_cluster_counts <- manual_grouped_drift_counts %>%
  group_by(PlotGroupID, HDBSCAN_cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n_clicks = sum(n_clicks, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    manual_group_coordinates,
    by = "PlotGroupID"
  )

cat("\nManual grouped cluster counts:\n")
print(manual_grouped_cluster_counts, n = Inf)

write.csv(
  manual_grouped_cluster_counts,
  file.path(map_table_dir, "manual_adrift_grouped_cluster_counts_improved.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Wide pie tables: including noise
# ------------------------------------------------------------

manual_grouped_pies_including_noise <- manual_grouped_cluster_counts %>%
  select(
    PlotGroupID,
    Latitude,
    Longitude,
    n_drifts_in_group,
    drifts_in_group,
    HDBSCAN_cluster,
    n_clicks
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = HDBSCAN_cluster,
    values_from = n_clicks,
    values_fill = 0,
    names_prefix = "cluster_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    EventID = PlotGroupID,
    pie_radius = 0.45
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Wide pie tables: excluding noise
# ------------------------------------------------------------

manual_grouped_pies_excluding_noise <- manual_grouped_cluster_counts %>%
  filter(HDBSCAN_cluster != "0") %>%
  select(
    PlotGroupID,
    Latitude,
    Longitude,
    n_drifts_in_group,
    drifts_in_group,
    HDBSCAN_cluster,
    n_clicks
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = HDBSCAN_cluster,
    values_from = n_clicks,
    values_fill = 0,
    names_prefix = "cluster_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    EventID = PlotGroupID,
    pie_radius = 0.45
  )

write.csv(
  manual_grouped_pies_including_noise,
  file.path(map_table_dir, "manual_adrift_grouped_pie_data_including_noise_improved.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  manual_grouped_pies_excluding_noise,
  file.path(map_table_dir, "manual_adrift_grouped_pie_data_excluding_noise_improved.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Map helper
# Same style as existing drift-level pie maps
# ------------------------------------------------------------

make_manual_grouped_pie_map <- function(pie_data, include_noise = TRUE, output_name) {
  
  cluster_cols <- names(pie_data)[stringr::str_detect(names(pie_data), "^cluster_")]
  
  cluster_cols <- cluster_cols[
    order(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove(cluster_cols, "cluster_")))
  ]
  
  pie_slices <- make_pie_slice_data(
    pie_data = pie_data,
    cluster_cols = cluster_cols,
    radius_col = "pie_radius"
  )
  
  xlim <- range(pie_data$Longitude, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-1.5, 1.5)
  ylim <- range(pie_data$Latitude, na.rm = TRUE) + c(-1.5, 1.5)
  
  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(
      data = world,
      fill = "gray92",
      color = "gray60"
    ) +
    geom_polygon(
      data = pie_slices,
      aes(
        x = Longitude,
        y = Latitude,
        group = piece_id,
        fill = cluster
      ),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.12,
      alpha = 0.95
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = cluster_colors_consistent,
      drop = FALSE
    ) +
    coord_sf(
      xlim = xlim,
      ylim = ylim,
      expand = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(
      title = ifelse(
        include_noise,
        "Manual ADRIFT Grouped Drift Pies: Including Noise",
        "Manual ADRIFT Grouped Drift Pies: Excluding Noise"
      ),
      subtitle = "Crowded ADRIFT drifts grouped near ~41N, ~38N, and ~36N for visualization only",
      x = "Longitude",
      y = "Latitude",
      fill = "HDBSCAN cluster"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(map_plot_dir, output_name),
    plot = p,
    width = 8.5,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
  return(p)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Make improved grouped pie maps
# ------------------------------------------------------------

p_manual_grouped_map_including_noise <- make_manual_grouped_pie_map(
  pie_data = manual_grouped_pies_including_noise,
  include_noise = TRUE,
  output_name = "hdbscan_manual_adrift_grouped_pie_map_including_noise_improved.png"
)

p_manual_grouped_map_excluding_noise <- make_manual_grouped_pie_map(
  pie_data = manual_grouped_pies_excluding_noise,
  include_noise = FALSE,
  output_name = "hdbscan_manual_adrift_grouped_pie_map_excluding_noise_improved.png"
)

print(p_manual_grouped_map_including_noise)
print(p_manual_grouped_map_excluding_noise)

cat("\nDONE: Improved manual ADRIFT grouped pie maps saved to:\n")
cat(map_plot_dir, "\n")

```

## Mean click spectra by HDBSCAN cluster with 95% CI ribbons

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Output folder
# ------------------------------------------------------------

minpts50_plot_dir <- "E:/Analysis/PCA_ICI_UMAP_HDBSCAN/Main_minPts50_5PC_scaled_logICI/plots"

dir.create(
  minpts50_plot_dir,
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Build 95% CI table from mean_spectra_minpts50
# ------------------------------------------------------------

mean_spectra_minpts50_ci <- mean_spectra_minpts50 %>%
  mutate(
    HDBSCAN_cluster = as.character(HDBSCAN_cluster),
    se_minmax_spectrum = sd_minmax_spectrum / sqrt(n_clicks),
    ci95_lower = mean_minmax_spectrum - 1.96 * se_minmax_spectrum,
    ci95_upper = mean_minmax_spectrum + 1.96 * se_minmax_spectrum,
    ci95_lower_plot = pmax(ci95_lower, 0),
    ci95_upper_plot = pmin(ci95_upper, 1)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    HDBSCAN_cluster %in% c("1", "2", "3", "4")
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Sanity check
# ------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\nClusters included in CI spectra figure:\n")

mean_spectra_minpts50_ci %>%
  distinct(HDBSCAN_cluster, n_clicks) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(HDBSCAN_cluster)) %>%
  print()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot: mean spectra with 95% CI ribbons
# ------------------------------------------------------------

p_mean_spectra_minpts50_ci <- ggplot(
  mean_spectra_minpts50_ci,
  aes(
    x = Frequency_kHz,
    y = mean_minmax_spectrum
  )
) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      ymin = ci95_lower_plot,
      ymax = ci95_upper_plot
    ),
    fill = "gray70",
    alpha = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 1.1,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ HDBSCAN_cluster,
    ncol = 2
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Click Spectra by HDBSCAN Cluster",
    subtitle = "Mean ± 95% CI; 5PC + scaled logICI; minPts = 50; noise excluded",
    x = "Frequency (kHz)",
    y = "Min-max normalized amplitude"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray85", color = "gray40"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(p_mean_spectra_minpts50_ci)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save figure and table
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    minpts50_plot_dir,
    "hdbscan_minPts50_mean_click_spectra_95CI_noise_excluded.png"
  ),
  plot = p_mean_spectra_minpts50_ci,
  width = 11,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    minpts50_plot_dir,
    "hdbscan_minPts50_mean_click_spectra_95CI_noise_excluded.pdf"
  ),
  plot = p_mean_spectra_minpts50_ci,
  width = 11,
  height = 8
)

write.csv(
  mean_spectra_minpts50_ci,
  file.path(
    minpts50_plot_dir,
    "hdbscan_minPts50_mean_click_spectra_95CI_noise_excluded.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("\nDONE: Mean click spectra with 95% CI saved to:\n")
cat(minpts50_plot_dir, "\n")

```

## Mean spectra with Variability Ribbons for All Non-Noise Clusters Color-coded to match maps / stacked plots

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Output folder
# ------------------------------------------------------------

minpts50_plot_dir <- "E:/Analysis/PCA_ICI_UMAP_HDBSCAN/Main_minPts50_5PC_scaled_logICI/plots"

dir.create(
  minpts50_plot_dir,
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Cluster colors used across maps / stacked plots
# ------------------------------------------------------------

cluster_colors_consistent <- c(
  "0" = "gray60",
  "1" = "#F8766D",
  "2" = "#7CAE00",
  "3" = "#1FBFCF",
  "4" = "#C77CFF"
)

cluster_colors_no_noise <- cluster_colors_consistent[c("1", "2", "3", "4")]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Use non-noise HDBSCAN clusters from final minPts50 analysis
# ------------------------------------------------------------

cluster_spectra_ribbon_data <- mean_spectra_minpts50 %>%
  mutate(
    HDBSCAN_cluster = as.character(HDBSCAN_cluster)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    HDBSCAN_cluster %in% c("1", "2", "3", "4")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    lower_sd = pmax(mean_minmax_spectrum - sd_minmax_spectrum, 0),
    upper_sd = pmin(mean_minmax_spectrum + sd_minmax_spectrum, 1)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Sanity check
# ------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\nClusters included in SD spectra figure:\n")

cluster_spectra_ribbon_data %>%
  distinct(HDBSCAN_cluster, n_clicks) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(HDBSCAN_cluster)) %>%
  print()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Faceted mean ± SD ribbons
# ------------------------------------------------------------

p_cluster_spectra_ribbons <- ggplot(
  cluster_spectra_ribbon_data,
  aes(
    x = Frequency_kHz,
    y = mean_minmax_spectrum
  )
) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      ymin = lower_sd,
      ymax = upper_sd,
      fill = HDBSCAN_cluster
    ),
    alpha = 0.25,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(color = HDBSCAN_cluster),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ HDBSCAN_cluster,
    ncol = 2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = cluster_colors_no_noise,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = cluster_colors_no_noise,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Spectra with Variability by HDBSCAN Cluster",
    subtitle = "Mean ± 1 SD; 5PC + scaled logICI; minPts = 50; noise excluded",
    x = "Frequency (kHz)",
    y = "Min-max normalized amplitude",
    fill = "HDBSCAN cluster",
    color = "HDBSCAN cluster"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray85", color = "gray40"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(p_cluster_spectra_ribbons)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    minpts50_plot_dir,
    "hdbscan_minPts50_mean_spectra_SD_colorcoded_noise_excluded.png"
  ),
  plot = p_cluster_spectra_ribbons,
  width = 10,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save ribbon data
# ------------------------------------------------------------

write.csv(
  cluster_spectra_ribbon_data,
  file = file.path(
    minpts50_plot_dir,
    "hdbscan_minPts50_mean_spectra_SD_colorcoded_noise_excluded.csv"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("\nDONE: Color-coded mean spectra with ±1 SD saved to:\n")
cat(minpts50_plot_dir, "\n")

```
